family.$description: One grotesque, one mono, one origin: Archivo (variable, poster DNA, wdth axis) for display AND body; Fragment Mono (a monospace derived from the Swiss grotesque form) for tokens, annotations, ordinals. Decision documented in the /system case study. Self-hosted via fontsource.

size.$description: Type scale with large contrasts between steps (Swiss typography). Steps 300-400 and 700-900 are fluid: clamp() interpolates between ~375px and ~1280px viewports; the rem term keeps browser zoom working (WCAG 1.4.4), and rem bounds keep the caps zoomable too. Reading sizes ramp gently (300: 16px to 18px, 400: 18px to 20px) — 16px stays the floor on narrow screens where larger type costs line length; labels and code (100-200) stay static.
